texto: Let me, however, although no verbal critic, protest against the profanation of the word friend. In this my history I must be honest, make a distinction between the oriental diamond and its worthless imitation of paste, and separate the grain from the chaff — gossamer words, that weigh nothing, from substantial realities heavier than gold.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Edward John Trelawny sobre temas ligados a amizade. A afirmação “Let me, however, although no verbal critic, protest against the profanation of the word friend. I…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amizade, friends, friendship-true-and-loyal, útil em momentos de dia-chuvoso e termino.

texto: I am one whose faith is, that love and friendship, with ardent natures, are like those trees of the torrid zone which yield fruit but once, and then die.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Edward John Trelawny: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. texto: Like a great fool, I went ashore with them, and they gave me some cursed stuff they called gin, - such blasphemy I never heard! At first when they told me they had set up a great distillery of gin, I thought them very useful, clever, good men; for you know, captain, any nation might be converted by hollands; -but this was the unchristianest beastliest liquor I ever tasted, and it made me - as I feel now. Yet the foolish, idiot-people of the island think it very good, because it makes them mad-drunk, and they believe Heaven sent it; but it made me believe the devil had got amongst them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Edward John Trelawny: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Like a great fool, I went ashore with them, and they gave me some cursed stuff they called gin, -…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, alcohol, gin pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de gin e spirits.

At first when they told me they had set up a great distillery of gin, I thought them very useful, clever, good men; for you know, captain, any nation might be converted by hollands; -but this was the unchristianest beastliest liquor I ever tasted, and it made me - as I feel now. Yet the foolish, idiot-people of the island think it very good, because it makes them mad-drunk, and they believe Heaven sent it; but it made me believe the devil had got amongst them.

texto: He is smitten on the brain, -he reads and writes verses! I caught him in the act! Fools might say he was inspired; but I know it is the first and worst symptom of lunacy. All other maniacs have lucid intervals; some are curable; but the madness of poets, dogs, and musicians, is past hope. Earth possesses no remedy, science no cure.
explicacao: Citação de Edward John Trelawny sobre temas ligados a motivacao. “He is smitten on the brain, -he reads and writes verses! I caught him in the act! Fools might say…” articula motivacao, in-love, love em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e dia-chuvoso.

texto: In my youth I loved climbing and scrambling up rocks and mountains: now I seldom intrude on the dweller of a second story, and my greatest enemy or friend may avoid me altogether on the third; so humbled is the aspiring spirit of my youth.
